entries.source_excerpt: hooks/hooks.json — all hook wirings use python3 X || python X; replace with the v2.16.0 detect-once pattern (commands/architect-team.md:175).
scenarios: hook wiring detect-once, no double invocation (A1)
acceptance_criteria: Every command in hooks/hooks.json uses $(command -v python3 || command -v python) and invokes its script exactly once
No hooks.json command contains the ' || python ' double-invocation form
tests/test_hooks_structure.py test_hooks_use_python3 + test_hooks_use_polyglot_python_fallback are rewritten to assert the detect-once contract and pass

entries.source_excerpt: hooks/vao_tools.py — three CLI tools crash with ModuleNotFoundError; wrap each lazy import in the dual-form try/except pattern (lines 61-68).
scenarios: the three VAO CLI tools run as a bare-module script (A2)
acceptance_criteria: python hooks/vao_tools.py verify-discipline-registry-current runs without ModuleNotFoundError
verify-inflight-clarifications-processed and verify-no-unilateral-override likewise run as bare-module scripts
each of the three lazy imports uses try: from hooks.X / except ImportError: from X

entries.source_excerpt: hooks/vao_tools.py:~4598 verify_no_pipeline_bypass checks /reviews/ against the RAW path; normalize backslashes + lowercase before the check.
scenarios: Windows backslash review paths are counted (A3)
acceptance_criteria: A ledger entry with a backslash review-evidence path is counted as a review
verify_no_pipeline_bypass does not emit a false independent-review-bypassed for that ledger
the path is normalized (backslash to slash + lowercase) before the /reviews/ check, matching the openspec check below it

entries.source_excerpt: hooks/inflight_inbox.py::mark_processed non-atomic rewrite can destroy a concurrent inject append; rewrite to temp + os.replace (run_metrics.py:184-186). Also safe_id the run_id.
scenarios: the in-flight inbox is atomic and path-safe (A4)
acceptance_criteria: mark_processed writes via temp file + os.replace (atomic on POSIX and Windows)
a concurrently-appended inbox line survives a mark_processed rewrite
a run_id containing /, backslash, leading ., or .. is rejected via safe_id before reaching the inbox filename

entries.source_excerpt: scripts/setup/teams_mode.py has no __main__/argparse but five commands invoke --banner --command; add a minimal argparse __main__ printing format_dispatch_banner().
scenarios: teams_mode and worktree_lifecycle expose the invoked CLIs (A5, A6)
acceptance_criteria: teams_mode.py --banner --command '/architect-team:inject' prints the dispatch banner
the CLI exits 0 even when the banner helper would otherwise raise (best-effort)
each of the five command invocations (inject, monitor-tests, visual-to-api, classify-test-prod-safety, discipline-status) produces banner output

entries.source_excerpt: scripts/setup/worktree_lifecycle.py has no CLI but two commands invoke cleanup-merged --against origin/main; add a minimal argparse __main__ with a cleanup-merged subcommand.
scenarios: teams_mode and worktree_lifecycle expose the invoked CLIs (A5, A6)
acceptance_criteria: worktree_lifecycle.py cleanup-merged --against origin/main --dry-run prints a one-line summary
the CLI exits 0 even on a cleanup error (v1.3.0 never-block rule)
the subcommand delegates to cleanup_merged_worktrees(against=, dry_run=)

entries.source_excerpt: scripts/setup/worktree_lifecycle.py 23 subprocess calls have text=True but no encoding= and no timeout=; add encoding + bounded timeouts. Same encoding treatment for worktree_paths.py, setup.py, pipeline-completion-audit.py.
scenarios: subprocess calls are encoding-safe and time-bounded (A7)
acceptance_criteria: every text-mode subprocess call in worktree_lifecycle.py / worktree_paths.py / setup.py / pipeline-completion-audit.py carries encoding='utf-8', errors='replace'
the network git ops (push ~810, push --delete ~852) carry a bounded timeout=
TimeoutExpired routes to the existing best-effort failure path (does not raise to the top)

entries.source_excerpt: Hook stdin decoding in four hooks uses the locale codec; a UTF-8 payload degrades the gate to a no-op on cp1252. Switch to sys.stdin.buffer.read().decode('utf-8','replace').
scenarios: hook stdin is decoded as UTF-8 (A8)
acceptance_criteria: pipeline-completion-audit.py, review-gate-task.py, teammate-idle-check.py, pretool_unilateral_override_guard.py decode stdin via sys.stdin.buffer.read().decode('utf-8','replace')
a UTF-8 payload (emoji in a task title) does not degrade the gate to a silent no-op under cp1252

entries.source_excerpt: review-gate-task.py:~151 and teammate-idle-check.py:~98 catch JSONDecodeError but not OSError on evidence read_text; a sharing-violation tracebacks the gate. Add OSError and treat as a blocking gap.
scenarios: an OSError on evidence read fails the gate closed (A9)
acceptance_criteria: review-gate-task.py and teammate-idle-check.py catch OSError alongside JSONDecodeError on the evidence read
an OSError is treated as a blocking gap identical to the missing-file branch
the gate does not traceback to exit 1 and silently skip

entries.source_excerpt: skill_invocation_audit.py:44-58 CANONICAL_COMMANDS lists 13 vs 19 real commands + 3 phantoms; regenerate to the 19 basenames + add a drift test. Fix slash-form false positive + prose-form 'architect team' false negative without broadening.
scenarios: CANONICAL_COMMANDS matches the real command set and the matcher is precise (A10)
acceptance_criteria: CANONICAL_COMMANDS equals exactly the 19 commands/*.md basenames; no mempalace-search/mempalace-status/code-review phantoms
a structural test asserts CANONICAL_COMMANDS == the live commands/ directory basenames
the slash matcher does not fire on /status inside a URL/path; the prose matcher fires on 'use my architect team'; exit semantics unchanged

entries.source_excerpt: commands/inject.md both python snippets import hooks.inflight_inbox with NO sys.path.insert; add it to every snippet + make ${MESSAGE} interpolation quote-robust.
scenarios: inject snippets resolve the plugin root and pass the message safely (B1)
acceptance_criteria: every python snippet in inject.md that imports hooks.inflight_inbox first inserts ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} onto sys.path
the message is passed via environment variable or stdin, not '''${MESSAGE}''' interpolation
tests/test_inject_command.py asserts the sys.path insert is present

Would have caught A1/A2/A5/A6/B1/C2.

acceptance_criteria: for every fenced python/python3 invocation in commands/*.md and every hooks.json command string, the target script is resolved and asserted to exist
scripts taking a subcommand/flag are executed with safe args and asserted to neither traceback nor silently no-op on an unknown argument

entries.source_excerpt: Keep the whole suite green under cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1.
scenarios: the suite is green under both encodings (E3)
acceptance_criteria: python -m pytest is fully green under default Windows cp1252
python -m pytest is fully green under PYTHONUTF8=1

acceptance_criteria.statement: The enforcement glue executes correctly (REQ-A): A1 hooks.json detect-once, A2 the 3 VAO CLIs run as scripts, A3 Windows paths match, A4 atomic + safe_id inbox, A5 teams_mode banner CLI, A6 worktree_lifecycle cleanup CLI, A7 subprocess encoding+timeout, A8 UTF-8 stdin, A9 OSError fails closed, A10 CANONICAL_COMMANDS == 19 + matcher fixes.

The command surface honors its own conventions (REQ-B): B1 inject sys.path + safe message, B2 ux-test preamble blocks, B3 detect-once sweep + tests, B4 setup allowed-tools, B5 absorb-phenotype anchored.

The skill bodies teach the code as it actually is (REQ-C): C1 schema v7 in all six locations + correct example, C2 plugin-root path, C3 unbounded residue reconciled, C4 MemPalace note authored, C5 Phase B3b resolved, C6 seven descriptions <1024 + cap test.

The documentation is reconciled and the version is bumped (REQ-D): D1 CLAUDE.md operative counts state pinned 4097 passed + 5 skipped / 163 test files (historical 3871->3920 narrative kept), D2 CODEBASE_MAP current + pinned counts, D3 README schema+HOOKS + tests badge tests-4097, D4 version 3.9.3 + CHANGELOG (3928->4097 delta) + INTEGRATION_MAP; all 7 COUNT-RECOUNT-AT-M7 sentinels deleted this cycle.

The suite is green incl. new regression tests under both encodings (REQ-E): E1 execute-the-glue family, E2 item-specific tests, E3 pytest green under cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1 with all new tests additive.

unit_test_targets.assertion: every command is detect-once; no ' || python ' double-invocation; same script both sides
run as a bare-module script without ModuleNotFoundError
a backslash review path is counted; no false independent-review-bypassed
atomic temp+os.replace; concurrent append survives; run_id with / or .. rejected via safe_id
--banner --command X prints the banner and exits 0 best-effort
cleanup-merged --against origin/main --dry-run prints a one-line summary, exit 0 on error
every text-mode subprocess call carries encoding='utf-8'; network git ops carry timeout=
OSError on evidence read is a blocking gap, not a traceback
== the 19 commands/*.md basenames; slash matcher ignores /status in a URL; prose matcher fires on 'use my architect team'

integration_test_targets.assertion: resolve every commands/*.md + hooks.json invocation; target exists; flag/subcommand scripts run with safe args without traceback and without a silent unknown-arg no-op
valid:true for review-remediation; the two pre-existing active changes stay green
green once under default Windows cp1252 and once under PYTHONUTF8=1

no stale OPERATIVE count: (1) zero COUNT-RECOUNT-AT-M7 sentinels in tree; (2) every operative total-tests claim states 4097 passed + 5 skipped; (3) every operative test-file-count claim states 163; (4) README badge reads tests-4097 passing; (5) any surviving 3871/3928 literal sits inside a version-history clause, never an operative current-state claim

out_of_scope: No live web testing, browser automation, or dev-server interaction — this plugin has no web surface.
No production system, deployed URL, or external service — none exist for this plugin.
No destructive git operations (stash / reset --hard / rebase / commit --amend / checkout other branch / clean -f).
No file outside the enumerated A-E scope; no broadening of a gate beyond its documented contract (A10 forbids broadening the matcher).
No new or changed runtime third-party dependency — Python stays stdlib-only.
